arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a Blazor Server web chatbot that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. The app uses Postgres (with pgvector) for storage and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to ground LLM answers in the actual docs.

## Approach — RAG Chatbot
1. **Extract** text from PDFs in the `Docs/` folder and split into chunks.
2. **Embed** each chunk using an OpenAI-compatible embedding model and store vectors in Postgres (pgvector).
3. At query time, **retrieve** the most relevant chunks via cosine similarity, then **generate** an answer with an LLM, citing the source document.
4. Serve the UI as a **Blazor Server** interactive chat page.

## Architecture

```
┌────────────┐  SSR / SignalR  ┌──────────────────┐
│  Browser   │ ◄─────────────► │  Blazor Server   │
└────────────┘                 │  (Chat UI)       │
                               └──────┬───────────┘
                                      │
                        ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
                        ▼             ▼             ▼
                  ┌──────────┐ ┌───────────┐ ┌───────────┐
                  │ Postgres │ │  OpenAI   │ │  Docs/    │
                  │ pgvector │ │  (LLM +   │ │  *.pdf    │
                  │          │ │  Embed)   │ │           │
                  └──────────┘ └───────────┘ └───────────┘
```

## Key Technology Choices

| Concern | Choice | Why |
|---|---|---|
| UI | Blazor Server (.NET 10) | Already scaffolded; real-time via SignalR |
| LLM / Embeddings | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI provider | First-party .NET abstraction; swappable providers |
| PDF parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig | Popular, MIT-licensed, pure .NET |
| Vector store | Postgres + pgvector via Npgsql + EF Core | User requested Postgres; pgvector avoids a second DB |
| ORM | EF Core + Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL | Standard .NET data-access |
| Chunking | Simple sentence-window chunker (custom) | Keeps it self-contained, no extra dependency |

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & NuGet packages
Add required NuGet references to `HelpChatbot.csproj`:
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` (LLM + embeddings)
- `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core + Postgres)
- `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)
- Blazor Server / component packages (built-in with SDK)

### 2. Configuration & secrets
- Add `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and OpenAI settings (API key placeholder, model names).
- Document required env vars / user-secrets.

### 3. EF Core DbContext & Postgres schema
Create `ChatbotDbContext` with tables:
- **DocumentChunks** — `Id`, `DocumentName`, `ChunkIndex`, `Content`, `Embedding` (vector)
- **ChatMessages** — `Id`, `SessionId`, `Role`, `Content`, `Timestamp`

Include a migration and ensure pgvector extension is enabled.

### 4. PDF ingestion service
`DocumentIngestionService`:
- Scan `Docs/` folder for PDFs.
- Extract text with PdfPig.
- Split into ~500-token overlapping chunks.
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`.
- Upsert chunks + vectors into Postgres.
- Expose an endpoint or startup hook to trigger ingestion.

### 5. RAG retrieval service
`RetrievalService`:
- Accept a user query string.
- Embed the query.
- Run a cosine-similarity search against `DocumentChunks` (top-K).
- Return matching chunks with metadata.

### 6. Chat service
`ChatService`:
- Maintain per-session conversation history (stored in `ChatMessages`).
- Build a prompt with system instructions + retrieved context + conversation history.
- Call `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync` and stream tokens back.

### 7. Blazor UI
- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — app shell.
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — chat page at `/`:
  - Scrollable message list (user / assistant bubbles).
  - Text input + send button.
  - Streaming response display.
  - Loading indicator.
- Basic CSS styling (no heavy framework needed).

### 8. Program.cs wiring
- Register services (DbContext, ingestion, retrieval, chat, AI clients).
- Map Blazor endpoints.
- Add a `/api/ingest` minimal-API endpoint to trigger document ingestion on demand.
- Configure the app to serve static files and Blazor.

### 9. Smoke test & verification
- `dotnet build` passes.
- App starts and renders the chat UI.
- Document the steps to run (Postgres setup, set API key, ingest, chat).

## Notes
- The plan uses **OpenAI** as the default LLM/embedding provider, but `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` makes it easy to swap to Azure OpenAI, Ollama, etc.
- pgvector cosine-similarity search is done via raw SQL (EF Core doesn't natively map vector operators yet) — kept in a clean repository method.
- Streaming is used for chat responses so the user sees tokens as they arrive.
- PDF ingestion is on-demand (hit an endpoint) rather than automatic, so it can be re-run when docs change.

2. **Embed** each chunk using an OpenAI-compatible embedding model and store vectors in Postgres (pgvector).
3. At query time, **retrieve** the most relevant chunks via cosine similarity, then **generate** an answer with an LLM, citing the source document.
4. Serve the UI as a **Blazor Server** interactive chat page.

## Architecture

┌────────────┐  SSR / SignalR  ┌──────────────────┐
│  Browser   │ ◄─────────────► │  Blazor Server   │
└────────────┘                 │  (Chat UI)       │
                               └──────┬───────────┘
                                      │
                        ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
                        ▼             ▼             ▼
                  ┌──────────┐ ┌───────────┐ ┌───────────┐
                  │ Postgres │ │  OpenAI   │ │  Docs/    │
                  │ pgvector │ │  (LLM +   │ │  *.pdf    │
                  │          │ │  Embed)   │ │           │
                  └──────────┘ └───────────┘ └───────────┘

+# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan
+
+## Problem
+Build a Blazor Server web chatbot that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. The app uses Postgres (with pgvector) for storage and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to ground LLM answers in the actual docs.
+
+## Approach — RAG Chatbot
+1. **Extract** text from PDFs in the `Docs/` folder and split into chunks.
+2. **Embed** each chunk using an OpenAI-compatible embedding model and store vectors in Postgres (pgvector).
+3. At query time, **retrieve** the most relevant chunks via cosine similarity, then **generate** an answer with an LLM, citing the source document.
+4. Serve the UI as a **Blazor Server** interactive chat page.
 
+## Architecture
+
+```
+┌────────────┐  SSR / SignalR  ┌──────────────────┐
+│  Browser   │ ◄─────────────► │  Blazor Server   │
+└────────────┘                 │  (Chat UI)       │
+                               └──────┬───────────┘
+                                      │
+                        ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
+                        ▼             ▼             ▼
+                  ┌──────────┐ ┌───────────┐ ┌───────────┐
+                  │ Postgres │ │  OpenAI   │ │  Docs/    │
+                  │ pgvector │ │  (LLM +   │ │  *.pdf    │
+                  │          │ │  Embed)   │ │           │
+                  └──────────┘ └───────────┘ └───────────┘
+```
+
+## Key Technology Choices
+
+| Concern | Choice | Why |
+|---|---|---|
+| UI | Blazor Server (.NET 10) | Already scaffolded; real-time via SignalR |
+| LLM / Embeddings | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI provider | First-party .NET abstraction; swappable providers |
+| PDF parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig | Popular, MIT-licensed, pure .NET |
+| Vector store | Postgres + pgvector via Npgsql + EF Core | User requested Postgres; pgvector avoids a second DB |
+| ORM | EF Core + Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL | Standard .NET data-access |
